title: CLI Quickstart For Embeddings
summary: Walk through the core `vai` embedding commands: model discovery, embedding generation, explainers, and similarity.
prerequisites: A valid `VOYAGE_API_KEY` is set in the environment.
commands: vai similarity "MongoDB is great" "MongoDB Atlas is amazing"

social.headline: A reproducible CLI walkthrough for core VAI embeddings workflows.
social.linkedinText: Powerful little demo from the VAI CLI gallery: model discovery, embeddings, explainers, and similarity in one reproducible workflow. The commands and source tape are included, so it is easy to run yourself.
social.xText: Reproducible VAI CLI demo: model discovery, embeddings, explainers, and similarity in one workflow. Commands + source tape included.
social.callToAction: Run it yourself and view the source tape.

voyageApi.python:
import voyageai

client = voyageai.Client()
result = client.embed(
    texts=['What is MongoDB Atlas?'],
    model='voyage-4-large',
    input_type='document',
)
voyageApi.curl:
curl https://api.voyageai.com/v1/embeddings \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VOYAGE_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "input": "What is MongoDB Atlas?",
    "model": "voyage-4-large",
    "input_type": "document"
  }'

mongoQuery.node: // No MongoDB query in this demo.\n// `vai embed` returns the vector directly and exits.
mongoQuery.python: # No MongoDB query in this demo.\n# `vai embed` returns the vector directly and exits.

explanations.vaiCommand: This is the one-liner developers see in the GIF. VAI wraps the request and prints the embedding without forcing you to write client code first.
explanations.voyageApi: Under the hood this is a single embeddings request to Voyage AI. input_type=document is used because the text is being embedded as source content rather than as a query.
explanations.mongoQuery: There is no MongoDB layer in this specific demo. The point here is to show the raw embedding workflow directly.
